text.title: Refactoring Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounded in Fowler 2nd ed. + Beck — behaviour-preserving discipline, scope-cap, tooling, and rollout cadence.

rule.statement: A `refactor:` commit MUST NOT change observable behaviour. New features and bug fixes go in `feat:` / `fix:` commits AFTER the refactor lands. CI rejects refactor commits whose tests change.

rationale.source: Beck — Test-Driven Development; Fowler — Refactoring 2nd ed.

rule.rationale: Two-hat rule. Mixing behaviour change with structural change makes review impossible: reviewer cannot tell if a side-effect was intentional or accidental.

rule.statement: Refactor cannot proceed unless the relevant test suite is green BEFORE and AFTER the transformation. If coverage is missing, add characterisation tests first as a separate commit; only then refactor.
rule.rationale: Without tests, behaviour preservation is a hope, not a guarantee. Characterisation tests pin current behaviour even if that behaviour is wrong — refactor stays safe; fixes come later.

rule.statement: One named transformation per commit (Extract Method, Rename, etc.). Multiple transformations require multiple commits. Commit message names the transformation: `refactor: Extract Method calculateTax from buildInvoice`.
rule.rationale: Per-transform commits give bisectable history and reviewable diffs. Bundling many transforms produces large diffs review skips.

rule.statement: A `refactor:` commit touches at most 5 files. Larger refactors split into a chain of commits OR escalate to [[code-decomposition-patterns]] / architecture-level work. Pre-commit hook `refactor-scope-guard.sh` blocks the cap.
rule.rationale: Refactor PRs >5 files lose 50% of reviewer engagement; reviewers skim. Smaller commits surface design questions early.

rule.statement: Renames use IDE / language-server refactoring tools (VSCode F2, IntelliJ Shift+F6, `rope`, `ts-morph`). Never sed/awk renames — they hit strings, comments, and partial-match identifiers.
rule.rationale: Language-server renames are AST-aware: they skip strings/comments and update only definition + references. Text replace silently corrupts strings + cross-language boundaries.

rule.statement: When refactoring data flow, prefer deriving values over storing them. Replace stored-then-synchronised fields with computed properties; Extract Method on the computation if reused.

rationale.source: Fowler — Refactoring — Replace Derived Variable with Query

rule.rationale: Stored-then-synced fields create staleness bugs. Computation on read costs CPU but eliminates the bug class; memoise only when measured slow.
